title: Delete a word, then repeat
summary: The dot command repeats your last change for free.

frames.caption: Cursor parked on 'speed'.
frames.narration: Three stat lines. The cursor is in Normal mode at the top of the buffer.

frames.caption: After dw — 'speed ' is gone.
frames.narration: dw is the delete operator (d) with the word motion (w). It removes from the cursor through the next word boundary, including trailing whitespace.

frames.caption: After j (down one line).
frames.narration: Move the cursor onto 'range' so the next change targets a different word.

frames.caption: After . (dot) — 'range ' is gone too.
frames.narration: Dot replays the last change verbatim. Vim remembers 'dw' and runs it again at the new cursor position. No keystroke wasted.
